judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 covers all four expectations, including the snapshot-staleness caveat that Report 1 omits entirely; Report 1 offers a valuable extra finding (score_batch.py is a non-functional stub) but misses expectation (d).

report_1.evidence: Full-run size, from data/manifest.json: 5200 transcripts × 1800 avg tokens/scoring call = 9,360,000 tokens needed. ... remaining: 640,000 tokens out of a window_total_tokens: 10,000,000 ... 640,000 remaining covers only ~355 transcripts (640,000 / 1,800) — about 6.8% of the 5,200-transcript job.

report_2.evidence: Full run size: transcript_count (5,200) × avg_tokens_per_scoring_call (1,800) = ~9,360,000 tokens ... Northpeak (primary/default): window total 10,000,000 tokens, remaining 640,000 ... 640,000 covers only ~6.8% of the 9.36M needed — a shortfall of about 8.72M tokens. Sending the default job here would exhaust the window almost immediately and fail to complete the run.

report_1.evidence: Route the run to ridgeline explicitly, since the default/primary path (northpeak) is budget-starved for tonight's window. ... invoking scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary directly

report_2.evidence: I would instead route the full run to the provider with headroom: python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary, which sends it to ridgeline/atlas-mini — the only backend that can plausibly absorb ~9.36M tokens tonight

report_1.evidence: Route the run to ridgeline explicitly, since the default/primary path (northpeak) is budget-starved for tonight's window. That means not using run_nightly.sh as-is (it hardcodes no --provider flag)

report_2.evidence: I would not run scripts/run_nightly.sh or the bare default command, since that silently routes the full 9.36M-token job at northpeak, which only has 640,000 tokens left — it would fail partway through and burn the little northpeak budget remains before any partial results are usable.

The usage_snapshot.json was generated at 19:25:00Z, about 3 hours before the stated current time (22:30 UTC). Per the project documentation project documentation's rule not to trust a stale signal as current, I would treat 640K/10.8M as approximate, not live, and reconfirm actual remaining balances (via the provider's dashboard/API) before kicking off the run
